articles.title: TECH COMPANIES
articles.summary: If you're looking to localize your marketing materials or expand globally, exploring AI translation should be a no brainer — it can cut costs up to 70%

articles.title: Register for the webinar [4] | Get the ebook
GEN ALPHA UNFILTERED: A DEEP DIVE INTO THE MINDS, VALUES, AND BEHAVIORS OF THE YOUNGEST GENERATION
articles.summary: Gen Alpha are shaping culture early through strong influence on family purchases and a growing sense of autonomy. They are spending more time offline, favoring real-world activities and physical toys, and showing greater interest in collaborative and creative gaming like Roblox, which has doubled in use since 2021. Girls are aiming higher in careers like law and medicine, but fewer boys believe they can do anything, revealing a growing confidence gap. Social media use is increasingly passive, with kids scrolling more and posting less, while influencer marketing is overtaking traditional advertising, even for food choices. Despite not holding the purse strings, many Gen Alpha kids, especially 8-11-year-olds, have decision-making power and are already active consumers, especially in ecommerce and virtual spending.

articles.title: HOW AND WHY TEENS USE AI COMPANIONS
articles.summary: AI companions on platforms like Character.AI, Replika, and CHAI have created new digital social spaces where 72% of teens have tried them for chatting, emotional support, role-playing, entertainment, and advice. Teens use AI companions because they find them entertaining, are curious about the technology, value their constant availability, and appreciate the nonjudgmental interaction that lets them share things they wouldn't tell friends or family. Many platforms allow easy access for users as young as 13. However, one-third of teens report feeling uncomfortable with AI responses.

articles.title: 5 B2B SOCIAL MEDIA STRATEGIES FOR SAAS LAUNCHES
articles.summary: The Gorgias team used 5 social tactics tailored for B2B to drive a 200% boost in engagement during launch week. They spotlighted the people behind the product to humanize AI content, diversified post formats with carousels, videos, and text to appeal to different preferences, and created ready-to-use assets for employees to amplify reach. They also lightly referenced trending content without relying on it, and enforced a one-week, no-links-on-LinkedIn policy to prioritize reach over immediate conversion.

articles.title: HOW BRANDS CAN USE UNCERTAINTY TO CREATE RELEVANCE
articles.summary: Due to a growing hunger for truth and confidence, consumers are turning to brands that can offer credible guidance and emotional support in a time of deep uncertainty. Traditional networks of trust, like family, community, and journalism, have weakened, creating space for AI to act as a surrogate source of authority, offering recommendations that often sound empathetic and informed. This shift raises the importance of consistent brand messaging across digital environments, where large language models are shaping public narratives based on the content they find.

articles.title: SKOOL
articles.summary: Skool is a community and course platform similar to Facebook Groups but built for creators and coaches who want a focused, ad-free space. It combines discussion forums, course hosting, progress tracking, and payments in one place, without relying on third-party tools. Members can interact, learn, and engage without the distractions of social media, making it ideal for those building paid or private communities.

articles.title: GOODBYE, FEATURED SNIPPETS: HOW SERP FEATURES HAVE EVOLVED IN THE AI ERA
articles.summary: AI Overviews surged from 3.93% to 27.43% visibility between January and June, while Featured snippets dropped from 15.41% to 5.53%. Sitelinks saw the largest gain, rising from 8.44% to 84.95%, likely due to changes in how Google displays Scroll-to Sitelinks. Other features that declined sharply include Shopping, Paid results, and Paid Sitelinks, all dropping more than 50%. Knowledge Cards, AI Overview Sitelinks, and Question SERP features also increased in visibility, though less dramatically.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM TESTS FRAME-SPECIFIC LIKE COUNTS FOR CAROUSEL POSTS
articles.summary: Instagram is testing a feature that shows like counts for each individual frame in a carousel post. The likes are tied to the frame visible when the user taps the like button, offering more detailed engagement insights, though not guaranteed to reflect frame-specific intent. This update follows Instagram's ongoing focus on carousels, which tend to outperform single-image posts due to higher interaction and resurfacing by the algorithm.

articles.title: GRAB YOUR POPCORN: HOW MOVIE FANDOM IMPACTS BUYING BEHAVIOR
articles.summary: Movie fandom influences buying behavior by creating emotional connections that last beyond the initial viewing. Amazon Ads and Crowd DNA identified 5 stages of engagement, from pre-release excitement to long-term community involvement, giving brands multiple points to connect with fans. Theatergoers seek immersive experiences, while at-home viewers value comfort and nostalgia, often pairing movies with food and related products.

articles.title: HOW KRAFT HEINZ LEANS INTO CULTURE TO CREATE MARKETING WINS
articles.summary: Kraft Heinz stands out by hijacking cultural moments. It partners with figures like music producer Mustard, parodies events like the Indy 500 with hot dog cars, and generates billions of earned impressions. The company taps into brand, cultural, and consumer truths to fuel creative ideas, prioritizing organic engagement and fast, iterative collaboration. By listening to consumers and reacting quickly, such as with A1's meme or Jet-Puffed's egg carton packaging, it drives both conversation and product sellouts.

articles.title: THE WORST DELIVERABILITY MYTHS YOU NEED TO KNOW
ARE YOU SURE YOU WANT TO USE AI FOR MARKETING?
articles.summary: AI-generated branding often produces safe and average outputs by relying on what is most common in large datasets, leading to predictable and repetitive designs.

articles.title: SHORTER VIDEO ADS ON FACEBOOK GET MORE REPLAYS VIA LOOPING
articles.summary: Facebook video ads that are 30 seconds or shorter loop repeatedly for up to 90 seconds, increasing total plays.
